title: carex elingamita
intro: Carex elingamita, also known as mountain kāpuka, is a type of sedge native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used by the Māori people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Carex elingamita include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Carex elingamita can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices for topical application, making it a versatile remedy in traditional Māori medicine.
